summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that JWTBearerTokenValidator with resource_server=None skips audience validation. A JWT access token intended for resource server A is correctly rejected when resource_server is set to B, but is incorrectly accepted when resource_server is None (the default used by JWTIntrospectionEndpoint).
expected_security_impact: A JWT access token intended for resource server A should be rejected by resource server B. However, when using JWTBearerTokenValidator with resource_server=None (as JWTIntrospectionEndpoint does), the token should be incorrectly accepted because audience validation is skipped.
actual_observed_result: The exploit script created a JWT with aud=https://api-a.example.com. With resource_server=https://api-a.example.com (correct), the token was accepted. With resource_server=https://api-b.example.com (wrong), the token was correctly rejected. With resource_server=None (JWTIntrospectionEndpoint default), the token was incorrectly accepted, confirming audience validation bypass.
verification_method: Three test cases are run: (1) correct resource_server - should accept, (2) wrong resource_server - should reject, (3) resource_server=None - should reject but incorrectly accepts. Test 3 passing demonstrates the vulnerability. The script exits with code 0 only if the vulnerability is confirmed.
evidence_produced_by_run: Test 1: Token with correct audience accepted (expected).
Test 2: Token with wrong audience rejected (expected, proves audience validation works when resource_server is set).
Test 3: Token with resource_server=None accepted (vulnerability - audience validation skipped).
The vulnerability is in claims.py validate_aud: when resource_server is None, aud_value evaluates as falsy and audience comparison is skipped at lines 149-155.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container.
